error.signature: AI tells someone in Bangladesh to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Bangladesh does not use 911. The national emergency number is 999, which connects police, fire, and ambulance. It is free and offers some English support.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Bangladesh expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Bangladesh. 999 is the national emergency number for all services. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 because Bangladesh is a large country
dead_ends.why_fails: Bangladesh's national emergency number is 999, not 911; a 911 call does not connect.

workarounds.action: Call 999 and state your thana (police station area) and district.
workarounds.how: 999 routes police, fire, and ambulance; give a clear local landmark or address.
